metadata.title: Vancomycin Powder Combined With Autogenous Bone Graft as a Prevention for Post-operative Infection for Spine Surgery

evidence.title: Vancomycin Powder Combined With Autogenous Bone Graft as a Prevention for Post-operative Infection for Spine Surgery
evidence.snippet: Title: Vancomycin Powder Combined With Autogenous Bone Graft as a Prevention for Post-operative Infection for Spine Surgery

evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet: Deep surgical site infection (DSSI) is one of the most challenging complications for spinal surgeons and can lead to a poor clinical outcome. This ambispective study was designed to examine the effect of vancomycin powder mixed with autogenous bone graft and bone substitute on preventing deep surgical site infection (DSSI) in degenerative lumbar fusion surgeries as well as any interference with bony fusion.

evidence.title: Study Status
evidence.snippet: Status: RECRUITING

evidence.title: Conditions & Keywords
evidence.snippet: Conditions: Spinal Fusion, Spinal Stenosis, Spondylolisthesis | Keywords: Deep surgical site infection, Vancomycin-impregnated bone graft, Spine surgery, Degenerative lumbar disorder

evidence.snippet: DRUG: without U-VANCO (vancomycin hydrochloride) - no vancomycin added

opinions.reasoning: Vancomycin is a well-established antibiotic that targets bacterial cell walls and is not a peptide.

Based on the information given about vancomycin hydrochloride (CHEMBL1200628), it is identified as a small molecule and not a peptide. The mechanism provided states that it acts as a peptidoglycan inhibitor, which further supports its classification as a non-peptide drug. Therefore, the primary intervention in this clinical trial does not involve a peptide therapeutic.
Both sources indicate that Vancomycin is a small molecule, not a peptide.

The trial investigates the use of vancomycin powder mixed with bone graft to prevent deep surgical site infections. While vancomycin is effective against bacteria, it is a small molecule antibiotic, not an antimicrobial peptide. It does not directly kill or disrupt pathogens through its own biochemical action.

The intervention involves vancomycin powder, which is an antimicrobial peptide that physically disrupts/kills microorganisms by inhibiting peptidoglycan synthesis in bacterial cell walls. This directly kills the pathogens and fits the definition of an AMP as it works through its own biochemical action to kill or disrupt pathogens.

The trial is investigating the use of vancomycin hydrochloride as a prevention for post-operative infection in spine surgery. Vancomycin is a peptidoglycan inhibitor, which directly disrupts bacterial membranes, making it an antimicrobial peptide. The trial's aim is to prevent deep surgical site infection, indicating that the intervention targets infection.

The published results indicate that the trial aimed to examine the effect of vancomycin powder mixed with autogenous bone graft on preventing deep surgical site infection (DSSI) in degenerative lumbar fusion surgeries. However, there is no evidence provided that this intervention was effective for its intended purpose. Instead, the abstract mentions "may decrease" rather than confirming a significant reduction in DSSI as compared to control groups. This suggests that the trial did not meet its p

The published results show that the trial failed to meet its endpoint of decreasing deep surgical site infections (DSSI) in elective lumbar instrumented fusion surgery for degenerative disorders. The study found no significant difference between the group using vancomycin powder mixed with autogenous bone graft and bone substitute, and the control group without vancomycin added.

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Deep surgical site infection (DSSI) is one of the most challenging complications for spinal surgeons and can lead to a poor clinical outcome. This ambispective study was designed to examine the effect of vancomycin powder mixed with autogenous bone graft and bone substitute on preventing deep surgical site infection (DSSI) in degenerative lumbar fusion surgeries as well as any interference with bony fusion.

interventions.name: with U-VANCO (vancomycin hydrochloride)
interventions.description: 1 gm or 2 gm mixed with autogenous cancellous bone graft

secondaryOutcomes.description: Vancomycin concentration was checked for both the serum and drainage.
Vancomycin concentration was checked for both the serum and drainage.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patients who have instrumented spine fusion surgery due to failed conservative treatment (bed rest, medication, rehabilitation programs) for 3 to 6 months
* Patients who are willing to sign the surgical permit after the surgeon explains all surgical procedures along with the surgical risks
* Patients who fully understand the surgical procedures as well as the surgical risks and are willing to sign the surgical permit and the inform consent
* Patients who agree one more blood withdrawal after operation

Exclusion Criteria:

* Patients who agree to accept the spine surgery but do not agree to sign the inform consent
* Patients who have allergic reaction to vancomycin

references.citation: Wang ST, Lin HH, Yao YC, Huang N, Hsiung W, Chang MC, Liu CL, Chou PH. Vancomycin powder mixed with autogenous bone graft and bone substitute may decrease the deep surgical site infections in elective lumbar instrumented fusion surgery for degenerative disorders: a prospective randomized study. Spine J. 2025 Sep;25(9):1866-1876. doi: 10.1016/j.spinee.2025.05.001. Epub 2025 May 5.

citations.title: VANCOMYCIN HYDROCHLORIDE - ChEMBL
citations.snippet:
Molecule: VANCOMYCIN HYDROCHLORIDE
Type: Small molecule
Max clinical phase: 4.0
